_status: SIGNED-OFF 2026-07-13 — owner-authorized go-live (3rd batch). Class I
name: Crooked Creek
description: Crooked Creek is a clear, gravel-bottomed Ozark stream running roughly 80 miles across Boone and Marion counties to the White River near Cotter. Designated by the Arkansas Game & Fish Commission as a Blue Ribbon Smallmouth Bass stream and a 22-mile Water Trail (Lower Pyatt to Yellville), it offers easy Class I floats past long gravel bars, riffles, fast chutes and deep pools, and is famed for feisty smallmouth bass. Entirely rain-fed and flashy, it drops low and skinny in summer and fall (and often goes dry east of Yellville) yet can rise into a dangerous torrent within hours after heavy rain.
managingAuthority: Arkansas Game and Fish Commission (AGFC) - Crooked Creek Water Trail; Fred Berry Conservation Education Center at Kelly's Slab
accessLaw: Arkansas: the public may float and wade streams capable of navigation. Use AGFC public access points (Pyatt, Snow, Mark Oliver, Kelly's Slab, Yellville City Park); the streambed and banks along stretches deemed non-navigable can be privately owned, so do not trespass above the ordinary high-water mark or portage across private land without permission.

gauges.name: Crooked Creek at Kelly Crossing at Yellville, AR

research.calibrationKeySource: AGFC Crooked Creek Water Trail + OzarkAnglers paddler-forum gage-height key (feet), cross-referenced with Float NWA cfs
research.calibrationNotes: GAUGE CORRECTION (safety-critical, incorporated): the originally-supplied ID 07056000 is 'Buffalo River near St. Joe, AR' (drainage 829 sq mi, Newton County) — a DIFFERENT river; verified verbatim against the USGS site service. Its live values (189 cfs / 3.39 ft on 2026-07-13) coincidentally resembled the expected numbers. The correct Crooked Creek gauge is USGS 07055607 'Crooked Creek at Kelly Crossing at Yellville, AR' (drainage 398 sq mi, Marion County; reports 00060 discharge + 00065 gage height), reading 203-205 cfs / 10.75-10.76 ft on 2026-07-13. All values here are on 07055607. LADDER UNIT = FEET (gage height) because the AGFC and local paddler keys are stated in feet on this gauge's datum (moderate flow ~10.5-12.5 ft; datum cross-confirmed: NWS Yellville read 10.80 ft and USACE Yellville ~10-11 ft the same week — do NOT reuse the Buffalo River gauge's ~3.4 ft datum). CFS CROSS-REFERENCE (Float NWA, on 07055607): ideal 250-1000 cfs; comfortable-floating minimum ~250 cfs, below which expect frequent gravel dragging. LIVE SANITY CHECK: today's 205 cfs / 10.76 ft sits just below the 250 cfs / ~11 ft comfortable minimum = low but floatable with some dragging (consistent with the summer draw-down). FLASHY: entirely rain-fed, rises fast; no NWS flood stage published, so treat rapid rises and any level above ~13.5 ft as dangerous regardless.
hazards: Kelly's Slab low-water bridge: a concrete slab crossing at the Fred Berry Conservation Education Center (just west/south of Yellville). Scout and portage; can be hazardous when water flows over the slab at higher levels. AGFC explicitly advises 'use caution at Kelley's Slab.'

Flash flooding: creek is entirely rain-dependent and flashy - 'can turn into a raging torrent very quickly, especially during heavy spring rains' and become 'too dangerous to float.' Check weather/gauge before and during a trip.
No official NWS flood stage is published for this gauge, so there is no automated flood alert threshold - judge rapid rises and any stage above ~13.5 ft as dangerous.
Strainers / log jams requiring portage, plus rocky shoals, fast chutes and low-hanging tree limbs (Class I-II hazards in places).
Summer/fall low water: gravel-bar dragging when the creek drops; the reach east of Yellville toward Cotter is intermittent and often nearly dry - not a reliable float.
